checklist.entry: Treat sampling as a client-provided capability where servers can request model completions through the client.
Require user visibility and approval controls where sampling affects user data, decisions or external actions.
Record what prompt is sent, what result the server can see and how prompt-injection risk is controlled.
Treat roots as client-exposed filesystem or URI boundaries that servers must respect.
Validate root URIs and prevent path traversal or access outside authorised boundaries.
Prompt users for consent before exposing roots to servers where applicable.
Use elicitation only where the client supports it and the request is associated with an originating client request.
Do not use form-mode elicitation to request passwords, API keys, access tokens or payment credentials.
Use URL-mode elicitation for sensitive interactions that must not pass through the MCP client.
Provide accept, decline and cancel handling paths.
Clearly identify the requesting server and target domain for URL mode.
